pattern: Each right/down move resolves through any chain of mirror reflections to a single landing cell, so counting paths becomes a DP over the empty cells in increasing row-plus-column order.
hints: The robot only ever rests on empty cells; a move into a mirror bounces (right becomes down, down becomes right) and may bounce again until it lands on an empty cell or leaves the grid.
Write a helper that, from an empty cell and a chosen direction, follows the reflection chain and returns the empty landing cell or reports going out of bounds.

Because every step and every reflection strictly increases row+column, process empty cells in that order, pushing each cell's path count forward to the (up to two) cells its right and down moves resolve to, all modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: a reflection runs off the grid and is not counted
mirror wall blocks all but forces a single route

stress.description: random grid with sparse mirrors keeping start and end empty
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(2, max(2, size))
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, size))
    grid = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.25 else 0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    grid[0][0] = 0
    grid[m - 1][n - 1] = 0
    return [grid]
stress.note: quarter density mirrors; corners forced empty per constraints
